task_completion.description: The agent locates and selects or interacts with the three specific invoice emails in the inbox.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent viewing the inbox, searching for, or highlighting the emails containing 'TS-9921', 'Cloud Hosting', and 'Catering' in their subjects.

task_completion.description: The agent enters the correct recipient email address.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the draft email window, the 'To:' field must contain exactly 'accounting@example-firm.com'.

task_completion.description: The agent enters the correct email subject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the draft email window, the 'Subject:' field must contain exactly 'March Invoices'.

task_completion.description: The agent types a brief message in the email body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main text area of the draft email must contain some newly typed text serving as a brief message.

task_completion.description: The three required emails are attached as intact files rather than forwarded inline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draft window must show an attachments section containing three files (often .eml format) corresponding to the 'TS-9921', 'Cloud Hosting', and 'Catering' invoices. The original email headers and bodies must NOT be pasted inline within the main message body.

task_completion.description: The email is kept as a draft and is not sent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draft window remains open at the end of the trajectory, or the email is visible in the 'Drafts' folder. There should be no 'Message sent' notification, and the 'Send' button must not have been clicked.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical email client or webmail interface to compose the email.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show a standard GUI email application (like Thunderbird, Outlook, or a webmail interface) being used, rather than a plain text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely attached files using the email client's UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The attachments must appear in the dedicated attachment UI element of the email client. The agent must not have simply typed 'Attachments: [filename]' into the email body to fake the attachment.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'mutt', 'mail', or python scripts being used to construct the email or manipulate the mail spool directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Expected Recipient: accounting@example-firm.com
Expected Subject: March Invoices
Expected Attachments: 'Invoice TS-9921 for Office Monitors', 'Cloud Hosting Invoice - March 2024', 'Catering Invoice - Q1 All-Hands'
Keywords to identify the correct emails: 'TS-9921', 'Cloud Hosting', 'Catering'
